skill.name: meta-results-risk-of-bias
skill.description: Generates the "Risk of Bias" results section for a meta-analysis based on assessment tables and statistics. Use when the user wants to draft the risk of bias analysis text from provided data tables.
skill.summary: Generates academic risk of bias results sections for meta-analysis papers from assessment data tables
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 36 potential issues across 4 files, all confirmed as false positives after human evaluation. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections were triggered by documentation text containing words like 'description' and 'describing' in YAML frontmatter, Python docstrings, and JSON audit data rather than actual cryptographic code. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections were standard Markdown inline code formatting (backticks around file paths and commands). The 'system reconnaissance' and 'hardcoded URL' flags matched benign documentation phrases and a legitimate source-repository link. The skill is a straightforward academic writing assistant with no network access, no credential handling, and no code execution beyond user-initiated Python scripts that perform text formatting.

low_findings.title: Documentation Source URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded GitHub URL in the source reference line at line 7. This is a documentation-only link to the skill's source repository and does not represent any active network calls in the skill code. No data is transmitted to this URL during skill execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 11 locations as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' across SKILL.md, Python scripts, and JSON audit data. All are false positives triggered by benign words like 'description' and 'describing' in documentation text, argparse help strings, and JSON audit fields. No cryptographic operations exist anywhere in this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Backtick Execution in Markdown Formatting
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 23 locations as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All are standard Markdown inline code spans (e.g., `scripts/format_result.py`) and fenced code blocks used to document commands. None represent actual backtick-based command execution at runtime.

content.user_title: Generate Risk of Bias Results for Meta-Analysis
content.value_statement: Drafting risk of bias sections for meta-analyses is time-consuming and error-prone when done manually. This skill automates the generation of professional, academic-quality bias assessment text from your data tables and statistics.
actual_capabilities: Generates professional academic risk of bias results sections from assessment data
Analyzes bias across five standard domains (D1-D5) with specific statistics
Supports both English and Chinese language output with localized figure labels
Automatically formats text with figure citations and captions
Validates inputs before execution to prevent incomplete or malformed outputs
Produces deterministic, reproducible output suitable for systematic reviews
limitations: Requires user-provided data tables and cannot generate statistics independently
Output quality depends on the completeness and accuracy of input assessment data
Limited to risk of bias analysis and does not cover other meta-analysis sections
Cannot verify the correctness of user-provided statistical values

use_cases.title: Medical Researcher Drafting Systematic Review
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher needs to write the risk of bias results section for a Cochrane-style systematic review. They provide assessment tables and the skill generates a publication-ready draft.
use_cases.target_user: Medical researchers and systematic review authors

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Writing Thesis Meta-Analysis
use_cases.description: A graduate student conducting a meta-analysis for their dissertation needs a properly formatted bias assessment section. The skill ensures academic standards and consistent formatting.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate students in health sciences and epidemiology

use_cases.title: Research Team Standardizing Review Outputs
use_cases.description: A research team producing multiple systematic reviews wants consistent risk of bias reporting across all publications. The skill enforces a uniform format and professional style.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Risk of Bias Section Generation
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with complete assessment data ready for drafting

prompt_templates.title: Domain-Focused Bias Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs detailed analysis of specific bias domains for discussion section

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Subgroup Bias Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: User comparing bias patterns between study subgroups or sensitivity analyses

prompt_templates.title: Full Journal-Ready Workflow with Formatting
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user preparing final manuscript for journal submission

output_examples.input: Generate a risk of bias summary for 15 studies where 20 percent were high risk and 80 percent were low risk
output_examples.output: Of the 15 included studies, 3 studies (20 percent) were rated as having a high overall risk of bias, while 12 studies (80 percent) were rated as having a low overall risk of bias. The main sources of potential bias were identified in the randomization process and measurement of the outcome domains.

output_examples.input: Analyze domain D3 specifically for the bias assessment across all studies
output_examples.output: For domain D3 (missing outcome data), 10 studies (66.7 percent) were rated as low risk of bias, 3 studies (20 percent) raised some concerns, and 2 studies (13.3 percent) were rated as high risk. The high-risk ratings in this domain were primarily due to attrition rates exceeding 20 percent without appropriate sensitivity analyses.

best_practices: Provide complete assessment tables with all five domains (D1-D5) for every included study to get the most thorough and accurate analysis
Review the generated text for numerical accuracy against your source data before including it in any publication or submission
Specify the target language explicitly when providing inputs to ensure figure labels and captions are correctly localized
anti_patterns: Do not use the generated text verbatim in publications without verifying every statistic and percentage against your original assessment data
Do not provide incomplete assessment data expecting the skill to fill gaps with reasonable defaults or estimates
Do not use this skill for sections other than risk of bias results, such as methods, discussion, or conclusions

faq.question: What input data does this skill require?
faq.answer: You need to provide the meta-analysis title, target language (English or Chinese), a statistics summary table showing bias risk distribution, and a detailed assessment table with scores for each study across domains D1 through D5.

faq.question: Can this skill generate bias assessment data from raw study data?
faq.answer: No. This skill formats and writes about existing bias assessment data that you have already completed. You must perform bias assessments using tools like the Cochrane Risk of Bias tool before using this skill.

faq.question: Which languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports English and Chinese output. Specify your language when providing inputs to receive correctly localized figure labels and captions that match your target publication.

faq.question: Does the output follow academic publication standards?
faq.answer: The generated text follows standard academic conventions for systematic reviews and meta-analyses. However, you should always verify the output against your specific target journal requirements before submission.

faq.question: Can I customize the figure citation format?
faq.answer: The formatting script inserts a standard figure citation and placeholder. If your journal requires a different citation format, you can modify the generated text after the script completes.

faq.question: What happens if my input data is incomplete?
faq.answer: The skill validates required inputs before execution. If mandatory fields or data are missing, it stops and explains exactly what information is needed before it can proceed with generation.
